methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $500K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Beyond Reach Labs is a pre-seed stage space and aerospace company founded by Mitchell Fogelson and Pele Collins. The target has raised $500K to date plus $175M+ in letters of intent. The valuation universe comprises 6 public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of three standard triangulation methods, only stage-progression analysis is applicable. This method uses the $500K raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to seed-stage progression, yielding a $1.25M implied valuation. Public comparable anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S of 25.8x across the five public comps cannot be operationalized without this figure. Private peer median analysis is similarly constrained by insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the space and aerospace sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.06M to $1.44M, centered at $1.25M. This narrow range reflects heavy reliance on a single method and material data gaps—specifically, absence of revenue and sparse same-stage comparables in the sector. The $175M+ in LOIs suggests significant downstream optionality, though letter-of-intent value cannot be included in current valuation. Key uncertainties include the timing and probability of LOI conversion and whether the capital intensity typical of space ventures will require material follow-on financing at materially different terms.
